2004-06-01.title: Bush Applauds Formation of New Iraqi Government
2004-06-01.abstract: President Bush described the event as a big step on Iraq's inexorable march toward freedom and democracy.

2004-06-01.title: Bomb in Baghdad Kills 3 Outside Kurdish Headquarters
2004-06-01.abstract: The blast occurred as the names of the president and cabinet for the new Iraqi interim government were announced.

2004-06-01.title: Indonesian Unapologetic On Expulsion
2004-06-01.abstract: Pres Megawati Sukarnoputri, in rare news conference, makes no apologies for her government's decision to expel Sidney Jones, leading American expert on Indonesia's terrorist groups; Jones has written series of reports on inner workings of militant Islamic group Jemaah Islamiyah that have been praised by law enforcement and intelligence officials in Washington; director of Indonesia's intelligence agency, ally of Megawati, publicly complained about Jones's reports, as did cabinet members; Megawati suggests that Indonesia's willingness to fight against terrorists, and matter of Jones, are separate; says technically she has not expelled Jones, only refused to renew her work permit (M)

2004-06-01.title: Grozny Journal; In a Ruined City, Even the Rubble Is Taken From Them
2004-06-01.abstract: Nine older women and two men who live inside shattered building in Grozny, Chechnya, have survived through war and cold and hunger and illness, pulling one another along if any lagged behind; little group of mismatched neighbors is living off rubble that has collapsed around them, cleaning and selling bricks, one by one, to new breed of entrepreneur in Grozny, brick dealer; photo; map (M)

2004-06-01.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Israel: Talks With Egypt On Gaza
2004-06-01.abstract: Israel's Prime Minister Ariel Sharon speaks with Egyptian President Hosni Mubarak about proposed pullout by Israel from Gaza Strip (S)

2004-06-02.title: Bombs, Bullets and Kidnappings: Just a Quiet Day in Iraq
2004-06-02.abstract: By the standards of most modern societies, today's events would have been labeled horrific. Iraqis saw it as a breather.

2004-06-02.title: Iran Still Making Nuclear Materials, U.N. Agency Says
2004-06-02.abstract: International Atomic Energy Agnency reports that Iran is still making parts and materials that could be used in manufacture of nuclear weapons, nearly two months after pledging to suspend nuclear program; Iranians insist they only want to produce commercial nuclear power, but agency report cites evidence that inspectors were misled, especially on where Iran obtained key components (M)

2004-06-02.title: The New Government Faces Bargaining Over Its Power
2004-06-02.abstract: New caretaker government in Iraq, which Pres Bush says will have full sovereignty, will have to negotiate sharp limits on that sovereignty in many vital areas, particularly security matters; presence of nearly 140,000 US troops and American diplomats virtually ensures that US will hold significant power (M)

2004-06-02.title: Ambush Kills 5 Aid Workers; Taliban Claims Responsibility
2004-06-02.abstract: The killing was another blow to the aid community in Afghanistan that has seen 32 aid workers killed since March last year.

2004-06-02.title: World Briefing | Africa: Zimbabwe: Plan To Censor E-Mail
2004-06-02.abstract: Zimbabwe government proposes legislation that would require Internet service providers to help trace and identify people sending messages that violate Zimbabwean law (S)

2004-06-03.title: 'Time Is Right to Move On,' C.I.A. Director Tells Employees
2004-06-03.abstract: Despite George J. Tenet's statement and the president's compliments, there was immediate speculation that there was much more behind the departure.

2004-06-03.title: Around Iraq, Fighting and Bombs Create Grisly Scenes
2004-06-03.abstract: Violence in Iraq may be increasing with approach of June 30 date for transfer of sovereignty, as American officials have warned; car bombing in Baghdad kills at least 5 Iraqis and wounds 38; outside Kirkuk, shell fired into arms depot at American military base touches off enormous explosion; in holy city of Kufa, fighting continues to rage between American soldiers and militiamen loyal to Moktada al-Sadr; photos (M)

2004-06-03.title: U.N. Envoy Wants New Iraq Government to Court Foes of Occupation
2004-06-03.abstract: United Nations special envoy Lakhdar Brahimi calls on new Iraqi government to broaden discussions to include Iraqis who oppose American occupation, and he suggests that his own authority in shaping new government has been sharply limited by American officials; suggests also that Americans are pursuing strategy in Iraq that relies too heavily on force and not enough on persuasion; calls American administrator L Paul Bremer III a 'dictator'; seems to stop just short of calling on United States to open talks with insurgents; Brahimi reportedly agreed to selection of Iyad Allawi as prime minister only after American officials pressured him (M)

2004-06-03.title: Sharon Is Confident Cabinet Will Pass His Gaza Plan
2004-06-03.abstract: Prime Min Ariel Sharon says he expects his deeply divided cabinet to give its approval for his broad plan to withdraw Israeli settlers and soldiers from Gaza Strip; solid majority of Israelis back plan, but Sharon's 23 cabinet members are split 12 to 11 against it; it is not clear any of them have changed their positions in recent days; photos (M)

2004-06-03.title: Despite Threats of Violence, Workers Struggle to Finish Projects in Iraq
2004-06-03.abstract: Engineers leading project to rebuild decrepit and looted base for Iraqi Army in Numaniya, Iraq, say threat of kind of violence that has slowed or stopped reconstruction projects around country will not keep base from being delivered on schedule sometime this month; engineers working on vast array of civil rebuilding projects around country are increasingly becoming target of attacks; resulting delays raise new questions about whether enormously expensive rebuilding plan will ever lift quality of life in Iraq enough to persuade ordinary Iraqi of America's good intentions and help create stable nation; photo (M)

2004-06-04.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Report On Juárez Killings
2004-06-04.abstract: Maria Lopez Urbina, who is special prosecutor assigned to investigate killings of at least 300 women in Ciudad Juarez, presents her initial report and cites gross negligence by 81 local officials; photo (S)

2004-06-04.title: IRAQ UPDATE
2004-06-04.abstract: Latest events in Iraq briefly noted; map (S)

2004-06-04.title: Paris Journal; Tennis Everyone? In France in Spring, All Come Running
2004-06-04.abstract: Two-week spring ritual known a French Open attracts world's best players, but sometimes action off court is more noteworthy than games played; 20-acre, 19-court site is at once upscale theme park and open-air corporate boardroom where deals are done, products are promoted, and Frenchness is celebrated; photos (M)

2004-06-04.title: Top Shiite Cleric Recognizes New Interim Iraqi Government
2004-06-04.abstract: Grand Ayatollah Ali al-Sistani acknowledged the selection of the new leaders but offered more caution than support.

2004-06-05.title: Germans Are Wary of Plan to Cut Back G.I.'s
2004-06-05.abstract: Pentagon proposal to redeploy thousands of US troops based in Germany and elsewhere in Europe does not come as surprise in Europe, but it has enhanced deep worries, especially in Germany, that plans, if carried out, would be seen as sign of weakened US engagement in Europe (M)

2004-06-05.title: 38 Filipinos are reported missing by rights group
2004-06-05.abstract: MANILA:—  At least 38 Filipino peasants, students, political activists and Muslims have been missing since January 2001, victims of "enforced disappearances" committed mainly by security forces in the government's campaign against suspected communists and terrorists, a human rights group said on Friday.

2004-06-05.title: U.N. Rights Chief Says Prison Abuse May Be War Crime
2004-06-05.abstract: Report by Bertrand Ramcharan, United Nations acting high commissioner for human rights, concludes that mistreatment of Iraqi prisoners by US soldiers could constitute war crime, and calls for immediate naming of 'international ombudsman or commissioner' to oversee situation (M)

2004-06-05.title: Bush Names Ex-Senator Ambassador To the U.N.
2004-06-05.abstract: Pres Bush names former Sen John C Danforth, who recently served as special envoy for peace talks in Sudan, to post of US ambassador to United Nations; current Amb John Negroponte has been confirmed by Senate as ambassador to Iraq (M)

2004-06-05.title: World Briefing | Asia: Pakistan: Another Missile Test
2004-06-05.abstract: Pakistani President Pervez Musharraf, witnessing succesful test-firing of missile capable of carrying nuclear warhead, issues reassurance that test is not intended as international political signal (S)

2004-06-06.title: For Bush and Chirac, It's a Cow Connection
2004-06-06.abstract: Pres George W Bush and French Pres Jacques Chirac strain to get along as Bush visits France for D-Day commemoration; Bush invites Chirac to visit Crawford, Tex, ranch to see some cows, perhaps without knowing that Chirac, as former agriculture minister, is a cow expert; Bush and Chirac still have differences over question of Iraqi security but seek common ground elsewhere, including need to ensure Iranian cooperation on its nuclear program; photo (M)

2004-06-06.title: Fighters Loyal to Radical Cleric Start Pullout From 2 Iraq Cities
2004-06-06.abstract: Fighters loyal to Moktada al-Sadr, Shiite radical who fashioned army from discontented of Iraq's slums, begin to withdraw from centers of Najaf and Kufa where they have been battling American forces since April; there are reports that Sadr has met with with Grand Ayatolla Ali al-Sistani, Iraq's most revered Shiite cleric; withdrawal, coupled with reported meeting of rival clerics, gives rise to hope that Sadr's rebellion might be ending; hundreds of Iraqis, including scores of civilians, have been killed since rebellion began and shrines have been damaged; Prime Min Iyad Allawi declares fighting is over, interview, and says Iraq will take control of thousands of Iraqi detainees held in American-controlled prisons; photo (M)

2004-06-06.title: Passage of Iraq Resolution Is Expected in Days
2004-06-06.abstract: Broad agreement has emerged in the United Nations Security Council on a U.S.-British resolution endorsing the interim Iraqi government, U.S. officials said.

2004-06-06.title: IRAQ UPDATE
2004-06-06.abstract: Update of events in Iraq on June 5; map (S) over setting up new Iraqi government; map (S)

2004-06-06.title: No Progress in Raising Troops By Rumsfeld in Bangladesh
2004-06-06.abstract: Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld, after attending conference on Asian security in Singapore, has hastily-arranged meeting with Bangladeshi officials, but is unsuccessful in getting troops from Bangladesh for service in Iraq or Afghanistan; Bangladesh currently has nearly 5,000 peacekeepers deployed around world on United Nations missions, more than any other nation (M)

2004-06-07.title: D-DAY / 60 YEARS LATER : In this series
Meetings Explore Egyptian Aid to Israeli on Gaza Withdrawal
2004-06-07.abstract: Israel and Egypt are trying to carve a role for Egyptian forces that would assist Israel with its proposed withdrawal from Gaza.

2004-06-07.title: SHARON ADVANCES TOWARD REMOVAL OF SOME SETTLERS
2004-06-07.abstract: Prime Min Ariel Sharon coaxes Israel toward evacuation of some settlements next year, winning approval from right-wing coalition government to begin preparing for what he calls 'disengagement' from Palestinians of West Bank and Gaza; government decision falls short of original evacuation plan, but through mix of compromise and cajoling, including abrupt dismissal of two far-right ministers before vote, Sharon appears to be advancing toward goal despite fervent right-wing opposition; advisers say vote will permit him to pursue plan while preserving coalition and forestalling parliamentary elections, buying him months to build more support; plan has opened fissures within Likud party as well as provoking outrage from many right-wingers; Palestinian Prime Min Ahmed Qurei says Palestinians welcome Israeli withdrawal if it is total and comprehensive and Bush administration calls plan 'courageous'; Sharon's actions come without seeking peace agreement; asserts Palestinian leadership is not credible negotiating partner but says Israel must act now to avoid ceding more land in eventual internationally imposed solution; Israeli court sentences Marwan Barghouti to five consecutive life terms, plus 40 years, for his involvement in attacks that killed four Israelis and Greek monk; on entering court, Barghouti calls for Palestinian uprising to continue; map; photos (M)

2004-06-07.title: A Perfidious Double-edged Strategy
2004-06-07.abstract: President Putin likes to be viewed as a crusader against corruption. However, the acceptance of perks and nepotism remain widespread in both the administration and government bureaucracy.

2004-06-07.title: New Wave of Repression Seen in Zimbabwe By-Election
2004-06-07.abstract: Party of Zimbabwean Pres Robert G Mugabe reportedly went to extraodinary lengths of intimidation and fraud to gain seat in Parliament of murdered opposition politician David Mpala in recent election in Lupane, region that has long reviled Mugabe's government; new campaign of repression seems to be building momentum with general parliamentary elections on horizon; map (M)

2004-06-08.title: U.S. May Cut Third of Troops In South Korea
2004-06-08.abstract: Bush administration gives South Korea detailed plan for withdrawing one-third of 37,000 US troops on Korean peninsula by end of 2005, as part of wider effort to reposition US forces around globe; removal of 12,500 troops would be first reduction since 1992 and would include previously announced transfer of brigade of about 3,600 soldiers to Iraq this summer; South Korean conservatives accuse Pres Roh Moo Hyun of allowing anti-American views to flourish, provoking US; photo; conservatives also say shift would surrender bargaining chip useful in nuclear arms talks with North Korea; Sec Donald Rumsfeld is reassessing strategies and new technologies worldwide

2004-06-08.title: Two Car Bombs, and Land Mines, Add to Casualties in Iraq
2004-06-08.abstract: Two car bombs exploded in different cities today, killing at least 14 Iraqis, but all the news in Iraq was not grim.

2004-06-08.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Suspected Cartel Bosses Captured
2004-06-08.abstract: Jorge Aureliano Felix and Efrain Perez Pazuengo, suspected senior members of Arellano Felix Drug Cartel based in Tijuana (Mexico), are arrested; both are wanted in Mexico and United States (S)

2004-06-08.title: Iraq and Middle East at Center of Economic Summit
2004-06-08.abstract: Pres Bush, secluded on heavily secured resort on Sea Island, Georgia, prepares for Group of 8 summit meeting at which he will discuss plans for encouraging Mideast democracy and keeping nuclear weapons out of terrorist hands; leading guest will be Iraq's new interim Pres Sheik Ghazi Ajil al-Yawar; security adviser Condoleezza Rice says UN Security Council talks on new Iraq resolution may be completed in time for meeting; photo (M)

2004-06-08.title: 2 Pro-Settler Members to Quit Sharon's Cabinet
2004-06-08.abstract: The announcement brought another note of disarray to the prime minister's plans for withdrawing from the Gaza Strip.

2004-06-09.title: D-Day / 60 YEARS LATER: Sacrifices in Normandy live on in the European memory
2004-06-09.abstract: Whenever I think of D-Day, it is not some Normandy beach that springs to mind but a peaceful French village about 15 kilometers inland. It is a pretty place called Fontaine-Henry, its houses built of a warm golden stone, and on one side of the village green a memorial to the dead of World War I. On the other there is a picture-postcard château, and next to it, the church. Walk through the gate and up the steps, turning left at the top. Underneath the yew tree, if you look closely, you will see that the churchyard wall is pitted with scars.

2004-06-09.title: U.S. Defends Plan to Reduce Forces in South Korea
2004-06-09.abstract: Bush administration insists that plans to withdraw one-third of 37,000 American troops will not be seen by North Korea as weakening of commitment to South Korea or as lessening of resolve to dismantle North Korean nuclear arsenal; photo; Deputy Under Sec Richard Lawless explains position (M)

2004-06-09.title: D-Day / 60 YEARS LATER: Out of the night sky like phantoms:Sainte-Mère-Eglise remembers
2004-06-09.abstract: SAINTE-MERE-EGLISE, France—  Sixty years is a long time, especially when measured in the seasonal rhythms of the Normandy countryside, of horses foaling and apples to be picked and pressed into cider. But for the elderly residents of this ancient village, at least for the handful of hardy survivors old enough to remember, the events of D-Day are so vivid they seem to have happened only a decade or so ago.

2004-06-09.title: Friends and Relatives Rally as a Top Russian's Bribery Trial Inches Forward
2004-06-09.abstract: Hearing in Moscow court moves Mikhail Khodorovsky closer to trial, eight months after oligarch was arrested at gunpoint on Siberian airfield, charged with defrauding government and evading taxes; family and friends appear in court to show support; photo (M)

2004-06-10.title: D-DAY / 60 years later : For past enemies, unity in horrors remembered in common
2004-06-10.abstract: RHYNERN, Germany—  There are miniature flags. Grainy photographs from the 1940s. Endless rows of titles on Normandy and war on the bookshelves. There are letters, certificates, pamphlets, maps and more photos. A painting of an armed speedboat and embroidery work from the Norman town of Bayeux adorn the walls.

2004-06-10.title: Despite a Pledge to Speed Work, Fixing an Internal Problem Takes Time at the C.I.A.
2004-06-10.abstract: Senior intelligence officials say Central Intelligence Agency has yet to put in place plan to address operational flaw rooted in practices that severely limit how much data about human sources is shared with analysts who produce intelligence assessments; Dir George J Tenet gave subordinates 30 days to devise solution in Feb after finding that problem contributed to errors in CIA's prewar estimates on Iraq; team headed by A B Krongard has only recently carried out pilot program that has not yet been broadly adopted; difficulty of devising solution reflects deep gulf between CIA's operations directorate, which recruits and supervises spies, and intelligence directorate, which draws broad conclusions from raw data from all sources (M)

2004-06-10.title: IRAQ UPDATE
2004-06-10.abstract: Roundup of events in Iraq; map (M)

2004-06-10.title: Bush's News Conference at Summit of Industrial Nations
2004-06-10.abstract: Text of President Bush's news conference on Thursday in Sea Island, Ga., as provided by eMediaMillWorks Inc.:

2004-06-10.title: With Potential for a Harsh Summer, Basic City Services Become the Priority
2004-06-10.abstract: Maj Gen Peter W Chiarelli, American commander responsible for Baghdad security and reconstruction, warns of long and violent summer but sees signs of progress; says one goal is to show residents how to conduct free and fair election; wants to test process with Oct vote for district advisory councils across city; has begun pushing projects to improve basic services; photo (M)

2004-06-11.title: Sabotage Cuts Power to More Than 100 Key Electrical Lines
2004-06-11.abstract: Internal Iraqi government report obtained by New York Times shows that sabotage attacks have cut power flowing through more than 100 of lines that form backbone of Iraq's electrical grid since US invasion last year; nearly 1,200 of huge support towers have been toppled; most of damage has been repaired, but far more attacks have occurred this year than were publicly reported; map; table (M)

2004-06-11.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Arrests In 2 Bombings
2004-06-11.abstract: Russian Deputy Prosecutor Sergei Frindinsky announces that two suspects have been arrested in connection with May 9 bombing in Grozny that killed pro-Moscow Chechen President Akhmad Kadyrov (S)

2004-06-11.title: 6 Acquitted in Killing of Russian Journalist
2004-06-11.abstract: Russian military court acquits all six defendants in 1994 killing of Dmitri Kholodov, investigative journalist who uncovered graft in armed forces (S)

2004-06-11.title: Rebel Cleric's Fighters Seize a Police Station in Najaf
2004-06-11.abstract: Iraqi militiamen loyal to Moktada al-Sadr seize police station in center of Najaf, release prisoners and allow looters to plunder building; photos; at least five people reported killed and 29 wounded in worst infraction of cease-fire (M)

2004-06-11.title: Cleric's Aide Backs New Iraq Government; Clashes Continue
2004-06-11.abstract: A representative of Moktada al-Sadr endorsed the new Iraqi government today, marking a reversal by the rebel cleric.

2004-06-12.title: In Shift, Cleric Backs Interim Setup for Iraq
2004-06-12.abstract: The rebel Shiite cleric Moktada al-Sadr, in a reversal of his past position, endorsed the interim Iraqi government and appeared to urge his followers to honor a week-old cease-fire that has been frayed by continuing violence.

2004-06-12.title: Names of the Dead
2004-06-12.abstract: Defense Dept confirms death of US soldier in Iraq (S)

2004-06-12.title: Nonpolitical Study of Terror Is Caught Up in Politics
2004-06-12.abstract: Terrorism task force of Council on Foreign Relations is scheduled to present report to Senate committee that commends Saudi Arabia for recent efforts to curtail terrorist financing, but asserts that opportunities for financing of terrorism persist; all members of task force endorsed report, which praised Bush administration's antiterrorism efforts but said they had not gone far enough; early draft of report became so mired in internal conflict over its tone that some members feared i t would never be published; report's authors are William F Wechsler and Lee S Wolosky, former members of Clinton administration; task force chmn is Maurice R Greenberg and vice chmn is Mallory Factor; they are major fund-raisers for Pres Bush (M)

2004-06-12.title: Philippine election results delayed
2004-06-12.abstract: MANILA:—  More than a month after the elections here, a new president and vice president have yet to be proclaimed as endless debates and filibustering delay the counting of the votes at the House of Representatives.

2004-06-12.title: White House Reconsiders Its Policy on Crisis in Sudan
2004-06-12.abstract: Bush administration is considering toughening its policy toward Sudan over events in Darfur, where thousands of people have been killed and over million driven from their homes by Arab militias that many in region say are linked to Sudanese Army; Bush officials are considering whether what is happening in Darfur amounts to genocide; Sec of State Colin L Powell says link between militias and government is strong, and that many people are still at risk of dying in Darfur and across bor der in Chad; map; photo (M)

2004-06-13.title: Russia Seeks Balance in Drug-Use Sentencing
2004-06-13.abstract: Russia liberalizes penalties for possession of small amounts of illegal drugs; makes possession of small amounts for personal use punishable by fine, though possession of larger amounts or trafficking is punishable by prison term; laws were already similar to those in West in theory, but actual sentences have been harsh even for possession of small amounts of drugs; special commission has redefined table of weights and penalties, bringing them closer to those accepted by world community (M)

2004-06-13.title: Road for Relief Team Is Gantlet of Enemy Fire
2004-06-13.abstract: Coalition Provisional Authority in Iraq has turned over scores of infrastructure and relief projects to Army; Army's ability to carry out projects amid deadly attacks may determine course of insurgency, which has been fueled in part by frustration with slow pace of reconstruction; profile of civil affairs officer Lt Eduardo Plascensia, whose company is overseeing low-cost projects intended to improve relations between American forces and Iraqis in Sadr City, while constantly dodging attacks by insurgents; photos (M)

2004-06-13.title: Amid Violence, Powell Braces Iraqis and Saudis
2004-06-13.abstract: After a weekend of violence in both Iraq and Saudi Arabia, Secretary of State Colin L. Powell warned today that Iraqis could face a long, bloody summer.

2004-06-13.title: Voters Reject Automatic Citizenship for Babies Born in Ireland
2004-06-13.abstract: Irish voters appear to have overwhelming voted to eliminate constitutional provision granting automatic citizenship to babies born in Ireland; Ireland is only European Union member state with such a rule; vote marks victory for government, which argued pregnant women come to Ireland so children will be entitled to European Union residence and welfare benefits; children born in Ireland of non-Irish parents will still have right to citizenship if at least one parent has lived there for three of previous four years (M)

2004-06-13.title: ERRORS ARE SEEN IN EARLY ATTACKS ON IRAQI LEADERS
2004-06-13.abstract: Senior military and intelligence officials say United States launched 50 failed airstrikes, far more than previously disclosed, and targeted broader array of Iraqi leaders during early days of war last year than has previously been admitted; say some strikes caused significant civilian casualties; say raids targeted 13 Iraqi leaders and were seemingly undercut by poor intelligence; targets included Gen Izzat Ibrahim and Maj Gen Rafi Abd al-Latif Tilfah, who now play leadership role in anti-American insurgency; photos (M)

2004-06-14.title: IRAQ UPDATE
2004-06-14.abstract: Update of events in Iraq; map (M)

2004-06-14.title: Shiite Cleric Is Forming Party That May Play Role in Elections
2004-06-14.abstract: Moktada al-Sadr, Shiite cleric whose fighters have waged 10-week insurgency against American-led occupation, is starting political party that will probably take part in Iraq's 2005 elections; Qais al-Khazali, Sadr spokesman, says Sadr cannot disband his militia because it is popular uprising, not organized force, interview; election move would defy American administrator L Paul Bremer III's edict barring people associated with illegal militias from taking part in elections in near future (M)

2004-06-14.title: Explosions Erupt in Baghdad, Killing at Least 12 Iraqis
2004-06-14.abstract: Powerful car bomb blast rocks central Baghdad, killing several people, believed to be American contract workers, riding in convoy of SUV's; crowd of Iraqis celebrates, shouting anti-American slogans; attack follows violent weekend in which suicide car bomber killed at least 12 Iraqis, 4 of them police officers, and wounded 13 others, Baghdad; separate car bomb exploded in Taji, killing American soldier and wounding two others; two Iraqi officials were assassinated over weekend; photos (M)

2004-06-14.title: Pakistan Detains Ex-Qaeda Head's Nephew and 9 With Terror Ties
2004-06-14.abstract: Pakistan reports arresting Mosabir Aroochi, nephew of Khalid Shaikh Mohammed, Al Qaeda's former operations chief, Karachi; says $1 million reward was posted for his arrest; arrests nine people said to be part of newly uncovered Qaeda-linked group, Jundullah, responsible for series of terror attacks in Karachi; says eight to nine other members are at large; arrests come as Pakistani Army scouts for hundreds of foreign militants in isolated tribal areas of northwestern Pakistan (M)

2004-06-14.title: Mandatory Tests Bolster Botswana's War on AIDS
2004-06-14.abstract: Success of AIDS clinic in Serowe, Botswana, hospital, is portend for nation, where nearly 4 in 10 adults are HIV positive, and most of rest of sub-Saharan Africa; Botswana, with world's highest HIV infection rate, has declared AIDS national emergency and hopes to eventually supply antiretroviral drugs to all who need them; began to turn tide since Jan, when Pres Festus Mogae ordered routine AIDS tests of everyone who comes into hospital or clinic; doctors say they are now catching people before they are seriously ill; map; graph; photos (M)

2004-06-15.title: A Glass Bubble That's Bringing Beijing to a Boil
2004-06-15.abstract: Chinese media and cultural community in Beijing are openly complaining about $300-million National Theater nearing completion near Tiananmen Square; photos; much-derided titanium and glass complex was designed by French architect Paul Andreu, subject of scrutiny since collapse of his terminal at de Gaulle Airport; government has still not lined up operating group as it remakes capital for 2008 Olympics, razing old neighborhoods and building new roads, subways and modern architectural monuments; local designers say projects by world's leading architects are overpriced, out of touch with Chinese history and inappropriate for poor country (M)

2004-06-15.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Muslim Graves Hit
2004-06-15.abstract: Fifty muslim graves are vandalized in Strasbourg (France) cemetary; Muslim and Jewish sites have been recent targets of hate-message graffiti in France (S)

2004-06-15.title: 4 Britons Charged With Abuse Will Face Trial in Military Court
2004-06-15.abstract: Four British soldiers will be tried in military court on charges of assaulting civilian Iraqi prisoners and forcing them to engage in sexual acts; attorney general, Lord Goldsmith, cites photographic evidence, which was turned over by photo-shop clerk (S)

2004-06-15.title: Israelis to Extend Barrier Deeper Into the West Bank
2004-06-15.abstract: Israel to build new segments of separation barrier around Jewish settlements, in deepest penetration yet into West Bank; barrier would enclose three sides of Ariel, with 20,000 residents; map; government says plan is what was agreed to with US, although US embassy raises questions about extension; Israeli missile strike on car in Nablus kills Khalil Marshoud, local leader of Al Aksa Martyrs; Arab courier is arrested, charged with bomb plot (M)

2004-06-15.title: Israel to Add to Barrier
2004-06-15.abstract: Israel is preparing to build new segments of its barrier around Ariel and other Jewish settlements that are more than 10 miles inside the West Bank. The move has drawn criticism from the Palestinians, and American officials have objected.    Article, Page A11.

2004-06-16.title: Letter from Europe : Too baffling to bother:Europe's disconnect
2004-06-16.abstract: BRUSSELS—  Two years ago, when Poland was rushing to finish the final details of its membership in the European Union, a Polish government minister described a frustrating problem he was having in his negotiations with Brussels.

2004-06-16.title: Violence in Kashmir Invades a Most Sacred Space
2004-06-16.abstract: Recent slaying of Maulvi Mushtaq Ahmad, shot in back while bent in prayer at Srinagar, Kashmir, mosque, seems to set new standard of venality in Kashmir conflict; Ahmad, 61, retired civil servant who long struggled for Kashmiri independence, was not enough of public figure to be target; shooting seen message to his cousin, Umar Farooq, spiritual and political leader of region's Sunni Muslims, who transgressed in eyes of hard-liners by opening dialogue with India; followed huge public rally that showed his popular support; map; photo (M)

2004-06-16.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Anti-Terror Police Arrest 12
2004-06-16.abstract: Twelve people are arrested in nine different locations north of Paris (France) for alleged involvement in terrorist activities; Islamic cleric is among those detained (S)

2004-06-16.title: With a Tap From Putin, a Leader Emerges for Chechen Presidency
Fox Seeks to Allow Mexicans Living Abroad to Vote in 2006
2004-06-16.abstract: Pres Vicente Fox to propose bill to let millions of Mexican citizens living abroad vote for president in 2006; most of Mexicans affected live in United States and Fox, who cannot run again, is courting them; plans visits this week to several cities where many Mexican-Americans and Mexican migrants live; all three major political factions back voting by Mexicans abroad; photo (M)

2004-06-17.title: Car Bomb Kills at Least 31 Outside Iraqi Army Base
2004-06-17.abstract: The bomb ripped into a throng of men waiting today to sign up for the new Iraqi army outside the army’s main recruiting station in Baghdad.

2004-06-17.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Prison For Framing In-Law As Terrorist
2004-06-17.abstract: Ahmed and Fatia Bechiri and three accomplices are convicted and sentenced to five months in prison for attempting to frame son-in-law Abderazak Besseghir by planting explosives and weapons in his car; Besseghir, who will recieve $18,000 in damages for mistaken arrest, worked at Charles de Gaulle Airport (Paris, France); photo (S)

2004-06-17.title: Bush Envoy Puts Latin Post, and a Stormy Past, Behind Him
2004-06-17.abstract: Otto J Reich resigns as Pres Bush's special envoy to Latin America; post has held little real power since region lost its foreign-policy prominence in crecent years; his career noted (M)

2004-06-17.title: Contractor Is Indicted for Prison Abuse in Afghanistan
2004-06-17.abstract: David A. Passaro is the first civilian to face criminal charges related to U.S. treatment of prisoners in Afghanistan and Iraq.

2004-06-18.title: Major Oil Pipeline in Iraq to Be Restored
2004-06-18.abstract: The assessment raised hopes that oil exports may be back to normal within a week if there are no further attacks.

2004-06-18.title: Pakistan Gunmen Kill Local Leader of Opposition Party
2004-06-18.abstract: Gunmen in Karachi kill official of Pakistan's leading opposition party, Munawar Soharwardi, in continuing political violence that has killed 71 people and unnerved sprawling commercial city; photo (S)

2004-06-18.title: Pakistan Says It Killed a Tribesman Who Aided Militants
2004-06-18.abstract: Pakistani officials said on Friday that they had killed a former Taliban fighter whose defiance of Pakistan's military had made him a folk hero among Islamic militants.

2004-06-18.title: Olympics Bill Does the High Jump, Leaving Some Greeks Low
2004-06-18.abstract: Greek government tells parliament that Olympics budget of $5.5 billion has been overshot by about $1.7 billion; eventual total could top $11 billion, sinking country into debt for more than decade; European Commission will issue recommendations on reining in budget (M)

2004-06-18.title: Rumsfeld Admits He Told Jailers to Keep Detainee in Iraq Out of Red Cross View
2004-06-18.abstract: Senior Pentagon officials acknowledge that suspected Iraqi terrorist who was kept in military jail, but kept off prison rosters, should have been registered more quickly with Red Cross, although they fault bureaucracy for seven-month delay; Sec Donald Rumsfeld says he issued secret-detention order at written request of CIA Dir George Tenet; says detainee, reportedly high-ranking officer of Ansar al-Islam terrorists, has been treated humanely; Senate Judiciary Committee rejects Democratic proposal to subpoena Justice Department records on prisoners (M)

2004-06-19.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: TV Chief Shot In Dagestan
2004-06-19.abstract: Dagestan (Russian) television executive Tagib Abdusamadov is critically injured by sniper's bullet in Makhachkala (S)

2004-06-19.title: Putin Says U.S. Was Alerted To Possible Attacks by Iraq
2004-06-19.abstract: Pres Vladimir V Putin says Russia gave intelligence reports to Bush administration suggesting that Saddam Hussein's government was preparing terrorist attacks in US or against US targets overseas; says Russia's intelligence services passed along information after Sept 11, 2001 attacks and before US-led invasion of Iraq began in March 2003; State Dept officials say they knew of no such information from Russia; Putin photo (M)

2004-06-19.title: Bush and McCain, Together, Call Iraq War a Conflict Between Good and Evil
2004-06-19.abstract: Sen John McCain and Pres Bush, in speeches to thousands of soldiers in Fort Lewis, Wash, present unusual joint defense of war in Iraq; call war conflict between good and evil that threatens existence of US; united front of president and his old political nemesis is striking development at time when Bush is under political siege for stating that ties between Iraq and Al Qaeda justified going to war against Saddem Hussein (M)

2004-06-19.title: Leaders of 9/11 Panel Ask Cheney for Reports That Would Support Iraq-Qaeda Ties
2004-06-19.abstract: 9/11 Commission chairman Thomas H Kean and vice chairman Lee H Hamilton ask Vice Pres Dick Cheney to turn over intelligence reports that would support Bush administration's insistence that there was close relationship between Saddam Hussein and Al Qaeda; say they want any information to back Cheney's suggestion that Mohamed Atta, ringleader of hijacking plot, might have met in Prague in April 2001 with Iraqi intelligence agent, meeting that panel's staff believes did not take place; Cheney photo (M)

2004-06-19.title: Israeli Platoon Leaders Fired Over Death
2004-06-19.abstract: Israeli Army dismisses commander and deputy commander of platoon whose gunfire killed Arafat Ibrahim Yaakub, Palestinian in wheelchair, on June 6 near Ramallah (S)

2004-06-20.title: Arroyo Has Big Lead in Philippine Vote Count
2004-06-20.abstract: The Philippine Congress concluded the counting of votes in the presidential election, with results showing the incumbent, Gloria Macapagal-Arroyo, winning.

2004-06-20.title: Sabotaged Pipeline Is Engineers' Nightmare
2004-06-20.abstract: Army Corps of Engineers and oil company workers struggle to deal with sabotaged pipeline near Zubayr, Iraq; suspect that only someone with expert knowledge of system could have placed explosives so effectively; attacks on pipelines can be devastating because many valves meant to isolate leaks are broken or missing; two recent attacks have shut down exports from Iraq's southern fields, which are by far its most productive; repairs on other pipeline are almost complete; photo (M)

2004-06-20.title: Canada Leader Loses Ground As Opponents Draw Closer
2004-06-20.abstract: Canadian Prime Min Paul Martin fares poorly in televised debates in advance of June 28 election; separatist Bloc Quebecois appears poised to sweep Quebec elections and New Democratic Party is doing so well in urban areas that neither main conservative nor liberal party is likely to win majority; Martin also faces unexpected threat from Conservative Stephen Harper, who has moderated rhetoric to broaden appeal; Martin's hope is that enough voters will turn away from New Democrats back to Liberals in order to stop Harper; photo (M)

2004-06-20.title: After Beheading, Rising Anger in New Jersey
2004-06-20.abstract: Beheading of Paul M Johnson Jr by Islamic militants in Saudi Arabia brings out displays of sympathy and grieving in his hometown of Little Egg Harbor, NJ, but one sign speaks of growing anger against Muslims; several townspeople say they share sentiment; Muslim officials around state and around world have been quick to condemn killing and offer sympathy to Johnson's family (M)

2004-06-20.title: Italian Police Look to a Cult In '98 Killings Of Teenagers
2004-06-20.abstract: Andrea Volpe, who was arrested on charges of shooting his girlfriend and burying her alive, leads Italian police to grave of two missing teenagers in woods at Busto Arsizio, northwest of Milan; Volpe and dead teenagers were members of heavy metal band Beasts of Satan; Volpe told police band members were killed in Satanic rite on Jan 17, 1998; police have arrested three other band members; police have widened investigation to look at other unsolved disappearances and killings in region; devil worship is growing problem among Italian young people; photo; map (M)

2004-06-21.title: Arroyo Said to Win Bitter Philippines Race
2004-06-21.abstract: Philippine Congress concludes that Pres Gloria Macapagal Arroyo has won re-election by more than million votes; counting votes from May 11 election took weeks that were marked by acrimonious debates; supporters of closest rival Fernando Poe Jr accuse administration of election fraud and threaten to contest results before Supreme Court (M)

2004-06-21.title: Militant Slain In Algeria; Ties to Qaeda Are Reported
2004-06-21.abstract: Algerian Army kills Nabil Sahraoui, leader of Salafist Group for Preaching and Combat, North Africa's largest and most dangerous Islamic terrorist organization; gun battle culminates intense government drive against Al Qaeda's affiliates in region; Sahraoui's death follows reports that group's founder Hassan Hattab is dead; Algerian military officer is quoted as saying group's leadership has been 'completely neutralized'; Sahraoui, also known as Abu Ibrahim Mustafa, was killed with four other senior members of group; photo (M)

2004-06-21.title: Saudis Seek American's Body as Militants Vow More Terror
2004-06-21.abstract: Saudi security forces comb Riyadh, looking for body of murdered American hostage Paul M Johnson Jr; concern grows that knowledge of his whereabouts died with killing of his captors; group that claimed responsibility for kidnapping, an Al Qaeda offshoot, vows to continue struggle despite death of its leader (M)

2004-06-21.title: Seoul to Send Troops to Iraq Despite Kidnapping
2004-06-21.abstract: As South Korean television broadcast pleas from a Korean hostage, officials said they would go ahead with plans to send 3,000 troops to Iraq.

2004-06-21.title: IRAQ GOVERNMENT CONSIDERS USING EMERGENCY RULE
2004-06-21.abstract: Iraq's newly appointed government considers imposing state of emergency that could involve curfews and ban on public demonstrations to counter violent resistance it faces even before it assumes power; Prime Min Iyad Allawi, who worked closely with Central Intelligence Agency in opposing Saddam Hussein's govenment in 1990's, says in his first news briefing that he will consider 'human rights principles and international law,' but that he intends to act quickly and forcefully against insurgency, using extraordinary methods if necessary; such measures could be applied in Falluja, where United States forces have been battling guerrilla fighters for several weeks, and Sadr City, Baghdad slum where three Iraqis were killed on June 20 in confrontations with American soldiers; other measures being considered by new Iraqi government include ban on public demonstrations, checkpoints and changes to search and seizure laws; photos (M)

2004-06-22.title: Heavy Fighting Erupts in Russian Region Bordering Chechnya
2004-06-22.abstract: Heavy fighting erupts in Russian republic of Ingushetia, region bordering Chechnya, as insurgents stage coordinated attacks on police and other security buildings in at leat three cities; official Russian Information Agency reports at least 13 people killed; others put death toll at 46, not including insurgents who may have died; fighting comes after Aslan Maskhadov, former elected president of Chechnya, now a prominent rebel field commander, said he decided to shift from guerrilla tactics to offensive operations directly against Russia's federal forces and pro-Moscow Chechen allies in region; map (M)

2004-06-22.title: World Briefing | Asia: Bangladesh: New Charges For Aid Group's Founder
2004-06-22.abstract: Bangladesh government charges Proshika, aid organization, founder Qazi Faruque Ahmed and six others in organization with sedition; Ahmed is already in jail on corruption charges and claims anti-Islamic fundamentalist government is harassing him with false charges (S)

2004-06-22.title: Iran Seizes 3 British Navy Boats and Detains 8 Sailors in a Territorial Dispute on Iraqi Border
2004-06-22.abstract: Iran seizes three small British Royal Navy boats and arrests all eight sailors on board, saying boats entered Iranian waters without permission; sailors were stopped at Shatt al Arab, stretch of water marking southern boundary between Iran and Iraq; navy was delivering boats to Iraqi Riverine Patrol Service; British officials seek to play down boat incident as relatively minor; arrests come during time of strained relations between Iran and Britain, mostly over war in Iraq and Iran's nuclear capacity; map (M)

2004-06-22.title: Bush Considers Replacing C.I.A. Chief More Quickly
2004-06-22.abstract: White House is said to be debating whether to act this summer to select permanent successor to George J Tenet, director of central intelligence who is stepping down on July 11; Pres Bush is reconsidering earlier plan to leave John McLaughlin in place as acting director through Nov election rather than risk confirmation battle in Senate this summer; people involved in discussion say there is concern that failure to select permanent successor may be both practical and political mistake; those being considered for post include Rep Porter J Goss, Rep Christopher Cox and Robert M Gates, director of central intelligence in first Bush administration; photos (M)

2004-06-23.title: F.B.I. Chief Sees NATO-like Antiterror Alliance
2004-06-23.abstract: FBI Dir Robert S Mueller III predicts bureau will someday be part of 'official international terrorism alliance' akin to NATO, speech to Council on Foreign Relations; reports preliminary talks among NATO members on forging more formal intelligence ties (S)

2004-06-23.title: 75 Die as Chechen Rebels Stage Raid Across Border
2004-06-23.abstract: Audacious overnight raid by heavily armed militants in southern Russian republic neighboring Chechnya kills at least 75 people and wounds dozens more; photos; fighters withdraw with minimal losses and cache of captured weapons; raid aimed at police and security posts in Nazran, Ingushetia, is largest by Chechen separatists since 1999; Pres Vladimir Putin flies to region (M)

2004-06-23.title: State Department Report Shows Increase in Terrorism
2004-06-23.abstract: State Department, in revised report, says number of significant international terrorism episodes rose slightly in 2003, and that number of those injured went up by more than fifty percent, contradicting Bush administration's earlier claims; reports 208 episodes and 3,646 injuries; Sec Colin Powell discusses report; chart (M)

2004-06-23.title: Spread of Polio in Africa Makes U.N. Fear a Major Epidemic
2004-06-23.abstract: WHO and Unicef warn that polio is surging in Nigeria and spreading to other countries, putting West and Central Africa on brink of largest epidemic in recent years; map showing number of cases since last year; 60 cases were reported by Nigeria in last week of April and Sudan is now tenth country with strain that originated in Nigeria; health organizations and Rotary International say goal of eradication by end of decade can still be met if funding is provided to immunize 74 million children in 22 African countries in synchronized program this fall (M)

2004-06-24.title: U.S. DROPS PLAN TO EXEMPT G.I.'S FROM U.N. COURT
2004-06-24.abstract: United States, bowing to broad opposition on United Nations Security Council, drops its effort to gain immunity for its troops from prosecution by International Criminal Court; outcome is political defeat for Washington; American diplomats, confident of obtaining routine 'technical rollover' of measure as they have in last two years, appear to have miscalculated impact of publicity given American mistreatment of Iraqi detainees; they were also caught off guard by Secretary Gen Kofi Annan's strong opposition; it will have no effect on vulnerability to prosecution of American soldiers in Iraq because neither US nor Iraq is member of tribunal, and its jurisdiction is limited to countries that do not themselves prosecute crimes by their military; Annan says decision by US not to pursue immunity resolution will help maintain unity of Security Council (M)

2004-06-24.title: Egyptian Aide in Talks on Future Security Role in Gaza
2004-06-24.abstract: Gen Omar Suleiman, director of Egyptian intelligence, visits top Israeli and Palestinian officials to work out precise role Egypt would play in overseeing security in Gaza Strip once Israel relinquishes coastal territory to Palestinians; Israel insists on managing security at Gaza's airport and seaport, while Egypt would prefer international forces there; Egypt wants to exercise some supervision over training of Palestinian security forces, and wants ministerial responsibility for security to be transferred from Palestinian Authority's chairman Yasir Arafat to Palestinian prime minister's office; Arafat has resisted both ideas so far (M)

2004-06-24.title: World Briefing | Americas: Colombia: Army Report Undercut
2004-06-24.abstract: Colombian Internal Affairs office report, obtained by New York Times, shows that soldiers who said they accidentally killed family of five peasants from long distance mistaking them for guerillas shot at least one from less than two feet away; Colombian President Alvaro Uribe has issued public apology for events that killed baby and three teenagers (S)

2004-06-24.title: World Briefing
2004-06-24.abstract: ASIA; MIDDLE EAST; EUROPE; AFRICA; AMERICAS

2004-06-24.title: Turkey Blast Won't Disrupt Bush's Visit
2004-06-24.abstract: There were some signs, meanwhile, that President Bush's continued quest for an explicit NATO role in Iraq might meet with success.

2004-06-25.title: World Briefing | Europe: Switzerland: Compensation From France
2004-06-25.abstract: Swiss Foreign Ministry reports that France has agreed to pay 3 million euros for damages caused by protesters at Group 8 summit at Evian (France) on border with Switzerland; payment is on top of 12 million euro payment France agreed to prior to summit to cover Swiss security concerns (S)

2004-06-25.title: World Briefing | Asia: China: Guarding Against Suicide Boats
2004-06-25.abstract: New China News Agency reports that Chinese Maritime Search and Rescue Center and China Ocean Shipping Company conducted joint operation simulating suicide bomb attack on tanker in South China Sea (S)

2004-06-25.title: Names of the Dead
2004-06-25.abstract: Defense Dept confirms deaths of three service members in Iraq, bringing total to 847 since start of war (S)

2004-06-25.title: Iraqis, Seeking Foes of Saudis, Contacted bin Laden, File Says
2004-06-25.abstract: American officials in Iraq obtain document showing that Iraqi intelligence agents had contacts with Osama bin Laden when he was in Sudan in mid-1990's as part of broad effort by Baghdad to work with organizations opposing Saudi ruling family; document states that Iraq agreed to rebroadcast anti-Saudi propaganda, but that request from bin Laden to begin joint operations against foreign forces in Saudi Arabia went unanswered; there is no further indication of collaboration; document is internal report by Iraqi intelligence service on its efforts to seek cooperation with several Saudi opposition groups, including bin Laden's organization before Al Qaeda had become full-fledged terrorist organization; Bush administration has said there was considerable evidence of ties between Iraq and Al Qaeda, but independent 9/11 commission concluded that contacts did not demonstrate 'collaborative relationship' between Iraq and Al Qaeda (M)

2004-06-25.title: Iran Frees British Servicemen Seized in Boats
2004-06-25.abstract: Iran frees eight British servicemen who spent three nights in Iranian custody after their boats strayed into Iranian waters; British diplomats meet with eight in southern Iran and fly with them to British Embassy in Tehran; photo (M)

2004-06-26.title: A Russian Fighting Hate Is Killed in Hate
2004-06-26.abstract: Nikolai M Girenko, Russian ethnologist who fought racist groups, was shot and killed by two young men in his St Petersburg apartment on June 19; wrote booklet to guide prosecutors and police officers investigating explosion of ethnically motivated crimes by skinheads, neo-Nazis and other hate groups; officials concede that he may have been killed for his work against these groups; photo (M)

2004-06-26.title: Out of Sight, Afghans Register Women to Vote
2004-06-26.abstract: Women in Afghanistan are registering to vote for first time, but strict rules of tribal culture are hampering process; women in conservative Muslim districts of southern provinces rarely leave their homes, and female registration teams must visit every household individually; whether women will vote in fall's parliamentary and presidential elections may depend on whether their husbands let them out of house; photos (M)

2004-06-26.title: Names of the Dead
2004-06-26.abstract: Defense Dept confirms death of US soldier in Iraq

2004-06-26.title: Army Used Speed and Might, Plus Cash, Against Shiite Rebel
2004-06-26.abstract: First Armored Division was given its final mission in Iraq in April: wrest control of string of southern towns from rebellious Shiite cleric Moktada al-Sadr's militia; division's operation is being studied by Army struggling to learn lessons of war that continues to evolve, even on eve of transfer of power; campaign was mix of military tactics, political maneuverings, media management and generous dollop of cash for rebuilding war-ravaged cities; formula could become model for future fighting against insurrections plaguing Iraq; Sadr remains at large, and question is whether tactical successes claimed by commanders have guaranteed lasting strategic victory; photos (M)

2004-06-27.title: 7 Militants Die In Israeli Raid In West Bank
2004-06-27.abstract: Israeli troops in Nablus kill Nayef Abu Sharkh of Al Aksa Martyrs Brigades, Sheikh Ibrahim, West Bank leader of Islamic Jihad, and five others, including one member of Hamas; men were found in hidden room and died in gunfight (M)

2004-06-27.title: Blast Kills 2 Afghan Women On Election Workers' Bus
2004-06-27.abstract: Taliban claims responsibility for bomb which destroys bus carrying election workers near Jalalabad, killing two women and wounding three people seriously, including one child; driver may have detonated bomb after parking outside military base; women were voter registration officials (M)

2004-06-27.title: Iraq Group Issues Threat to Behead a Missing Marine
2004-06-27.abstract: A militant Iraqi group said it would behead an American marine unless the U.S. released all Iraqi prisoners.

2004-06-27.title: Despite an Act of Leniency, China Has Its Eye on the Web
2004-06-27.abstract: China gives Internet democracy advocate Du Daobin suspended sentence; some applaud show of leniency, but others warn that magnanimity conceals push for tighter controls; estimated 30,000 Internet police monitor users, blocking sites and killing discussion threads on bulletin boards; provincial governments are adding their own controls; Internet bar users in Shanghai must now swipe cards with their national identity number to track use; analysts believe China is deeply concerned about blogging as medium of expression; photo (M)

2004-06-27.title: Amid Protests, Bush Sees Thaw in Europe Over Iraq
2004-06-27.abstract: Pres Bush, in Ireland for annual United States-European Union summit, says 'bitter differences' between United States and Europe over war in Iraq are in past, but antiwar protests delay news conference; Bush hopes NATO will agree at summit in Istanbul to help train Iraqi security forces; 10,000 protesters march in Dublin prior to meeting; 6,000 soldiers and police dug trenches and threw up earth embankments around meeting site to discourage protesters; photo (M)

2004-06-28.title: Handover Completed Early to Thwart Attacks, Officials Say
2004-06-28.abstract: The official transfer was moved up by two days in an apparent bid to surprise insurgents who may have tried to sabotage the step toward self rule.

2004-06-28.title: India and Pakistan Report Little Progress as Talks Resume
2004-06-28.abstract: But the two sides did announce more measures to build confidence between the nuclear-armed neighbors.

2004-06-28.title: Extent of Government's Control Unclear as It Formally Steps In
2004-06-28.abstract: By shifting power two days early, officials hoped to foil attacks by insurgents whom U.S. forces are still struggling to vanquish.

2004-06-28.title: U.S.-Led Forces Would Back Martial Law, Bush Says
2004-06-28.abstract: President Bush said today that coalition forces in Iraq would support a possible decision by the new Iraqi leadership to declare martial law to deal with escalating violence and terror attacks.

2004-06-28.title: Guerrilla Raids Force Chechen Refugees to Flee Again
2004-06-28.abstract: Chechen refugees in republic of Ingushetia say they are being blamed for success of Islamic guerrillas who overran police station and checkpoints last week, systematically executing law enforcement and military personnel who tried to stop them; Russian and Ingush police are accusing Chechen refugees of assisting and sheltering guerrillas; thousands of Chechens are fleeing Ingushetia for Grozny, heeding implicit message that to stay is to risk their lives; photo; map (M)

2004-06-29.title: At a Baghdad Hospital, Fear and Resignation Remain on an Unexpectedly Ordinary Day
2004-06-29.abstract: Scene inside hospital in Baghdad is portrait of weariness on day Americans cede formal sovereignty to Iraqis; neither doctors nor patients have great expectations for new government; many patients are civilians, adults and children hit by stray bullets and rockets on streets of Baghdad; photo (M)

2004-06-29.title: U.S. to Aid Antiterror Plan in Philippines
2004-06-29.abstract: United States and Philippines will hold joint military exercises in Philippines in counterterrorism training program aimed at destroying local cells of Jemaah Islamiyah, Southeast Asian network with links to Al Qaeda (S)

2004-06-29.title: Tactic of Delayed Miranda Warning Is Barred
2004-06-29.abstract: Supreme Court rejects police interrogation tactic designed to induce suspects to give incriminating statements after purposely delaying Miranda warnings; tactic has been growing in popularity and is taught in police training courses; court's 5-to-4 decision against strategy invalidates murder case in Missouri (M)

2004-06-29.title: Israelis Roll Into Gaza to Find Rocket Sites
2004-06-29.abstract: Israeli tanks rolled into the northeastern corner of the Gaza Strip today with the aim of silencing Palestinian rocket fire.

2004-06-29.title: Parting Words From Bremer, Acceptance From Allawi and Congratulations From Bush
2004-06-29.abstract: Text of L Paul Bremer III's farewell letter to members of Iraq's Coalition Provisional Authority (M)

2004-06-30.title: World Briefing | Americas: Paraguay: Former Strongman Returns
2004-06-30.abstract: Gen Lino Oviedo, former military commander and presidential contender, returns to Paraguay after over five years in exile to contest criminal charges against him; was sentenced to 10 years in prison for leading 1996 coup attempt and faces trial on other charges (S)

2004-06-30.title: Bush Urges All Autocrats To Yield Now To Democracy
2004-06-30.abstract: Pres Bush calls on Muslim nations to embrace democracy, warning Mideast autocrats, including American allies, must recognize 'direction of events of the day,' speech at end of NATO summit meeting, Istanbul; again stresses need to confront terrorism by forcefully encouraging Muslim nations to modernize; themes of democracy and terrorism infused much of discussion at meeting; Turkish officials say package containing plastic explosive exploded on Turkish Airlines plane parked at Istanbul airport, injuring two workers; NATO leaders agree to send up to 3,500 more troops to Afghanistan, but reject Pres Hamid Karzai's plea to send soldiers now; photo (M)

2004-06-30.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Iran Project No Worry, Nuclear Chief Says
2004-06-30.abstract: International Atomic Energy Agency head Mohamed ElBaradei says Russia's project to help Iran build civilian nuclear reactor is not cause for international concern, even though agency probes charges that Iran secretly develops nuclear arms, meeting with Russian Pres Vladimir V Putin, near Moscow; effectively endorses Russia's plans to insist that Iran return any spent nuclear fuel at plant (S)

2004-06-30.title: China Tries to Block News of Hong Kong's Pro-Democracy March
2004-06-30.abstract: China is clamping down on travel by Chinese tourists to Hong Kong to prevent news of expected huge pro-democracy march in Hong Kong from spreading on mainland; march will protest China's decision not to allow general elections in Hong Kong; Hong Kong's leading democrats are split over how aggressively to challenge China (M)

2004-06-30.title: World Briefing | Europe: European Court Backs Ban On Muslim Scarves
2004-06-30.abstract: European Court of Human Rights rejects appeal by Turkish student barred from attending Istanbul university wearing head scarf; holds ban is not discriminatory (S)
